gateRules.description: Cold water safety for ALL watercraft activities
gateRules.trigger: any watercraft use, launch, paddling scenario
gateRules.action: GATE -- include cold water shock warning (gasping reflex, incapacitation within minutes in cold water), PFD requirement, and buddy system
GATE -- structural safety check: frame integrity, skin/bark attachment, flotation characteristics

gateRules.description: Tool safety for construction tools (crooked knife, awl, adze)
gateRules.trigger: use of traditional woodworking and construction tools
gateRules.action: GATE -- include tool safety: cutting direction, sharp tool handling, secure work surface

gateRules.trigger: capsizing, falling through ice, water entry scenarios
gateRules.action: GATE -- include 1-10-1 rule (1 minute to control breathing, 10 minutes of meaningful movement, 1 hour before unconsciousness in cold water)

annotateRules.action: ANNOTATE -- check weather, wind, and water conditions before launch

safetyNotes.coldWaterShock: Cold water shock triggers an involuntary gasping reflex upon immersion. Even strong swimmers can inhale water and drown within seconds. Water below 15°C (59°F) causes swimming failure within 3-30 minutes. Always wear a PFD, always paddle with a buddy, always know the 1-10-1 rule: 1 minute to control breathing, 10 minutes of meaningful movement, 1 hour before unconsciousness.
safetyNotes.structuralIntegrity: Traditional watercraft (birchbark canoe, qajaq, umiak) require careful inspection before use. Check frame joints, skin/bark attachment, and waterproofing. A structurally compromised craft can fail suddenly in cold water with life-threatening consequences.
safetyNotes.toolSafety: Traditional construction tools (crooked knife, awl, bone adze) are extremely sharp. Always cut away from the body, maintain a secure work surface, and keep blades covered when not in use. Seek hands-on instruction from an experienced craftsperson before attempting traditional tool use.
